name: Chocolate Mint Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
1 Melt half a cup of mint-chocolate chips over hot (not boiling) water until smooth. Let cool to room temperature and set aside.
2 In a large bowl, combine butter and sugar; beat until creamy. Add egg and vanilla extract, blending well.
3 Gradually add flour and salt. Place one cup of the dough in a small bowl. Add melted chips and blend thoroughly. Shape into a ball and flatten.
4 Cover with plastic wrap. Shape remaining dough into a ball and flatten. Cover with plastic wrap. Chill until firm (about 1 1/2 hours).
5 Preheat oven to 375°F. Between sheets of waxed paper, roll each ball of dough into a 13 x 9-inch rectangle.
6 Remove top layers of waxed paper and invert the chocolate dough onto the plain dough. Peel off waxed paper.
7 Starting from the long side, roll up into a jellyroll shape. Cut into 1/4-inch slices.
8 Place on an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 375°F for 7 to 8 minutes. Cool completely on wire racks.
9 Melt remaining chips over hot (not boiling) water until smooth. Spread flat side of each cookie with a slightly rounded teaspoonful of melted chocolate. Chill until set.
